fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 70 uses strict greater-than (`>`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>=`), so when `len(recent) == limit` the function incorrectly allows the request instead of denying it.

constraints.intent: When len(recent) equals limit the branch must be taken (denied); the condition should be >= not >.
In the else branch (allowed=True), the number of recent events must be strictly less than the limit.
When recent count equals limit, the rate limiter must deny the request (remaining==0, allowed==False).
When allowed is True, remaining must be positive; when allowed is False, remaining must be 0.
